title: The One Where Ross Is Fine

lines.text: Joey and Rachel's apartment. The scene starts where we took off in the last episode with Ross seeing Joey and Rachel kissing. Rosss tares at them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, Ross, I realise that you didn't expect to walk in and see that, but.. Let me explain, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We weren't doing anything!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach, he just saw us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shhh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what you saw, that is the extent of it, okay? One kiss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, come on, that is a lie. We also kissed in Barbados.
Joey (to Rachel): Dude, chill! (to Ross) Okay, we also kissed in Barbados, but we didn't plan it, okay? And the only reason that that happened was because I saw you kissing Charlie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you started it! I've got to chill.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, we probably should have talked to you about this before it ever happened, but..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We feel so terrible about this, Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but it did happen, so...
Ross looks shocked and says nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross? (to Joey) Can we just close the door?
OPENING CREDITS
Joey and Rachel's apartment. Continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, say something. Anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you two are..?

lines.speaker: Joey and Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And have you .. ed?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But if I hadn't walked in here, would you..?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Probably.
Rachel looks at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, this is not how we wanted you to find out about this. You have every right to go nuts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm not going nuts. Do you see me go nuts?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, but you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey... If you two are happy, then I'm happy for you. (Squeaky.) I'm fine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Absolutely. (Very Squeaky.) I'm fine! Totally fine. I don't know why it's coming out all loud and squeaky, 'cause really, (deep voice) I'm fine. I'm not saying I wasn't a little surprised to see you guys kissing. I mean, at first I was like.. (Screams.) But now that I've had time to absorb it; Lovin' this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross..

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's all working out! Me & Charlie, and you two. You know what we should do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Calm ourselves?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. We should all have dinner. Yes, we'll do it tomorrow night. I'll cook!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, don't you think that will be a little weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Weird? What? What's weird? The only thing weird would be if someone didn't like Mexican food, because I'm making fajitas!! (Storms off.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do like fajitas.
Central Perk. Monica and Chandler are there. They have lots of brochures about adoption in front of them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, this adoption stuff is so overwhelming. There's inter-country adoption, dependency adoption.. There are so many ways to go, and this is like the biggest decision of our lives.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's a hair in my coffee.
Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, have you seen Frank Jr., 'cause he's meeting me here with the triplets.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, it's funny. Every time you say "triplets," I immediately think of three hot blonde 19-year olds.
Monica glares at him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's sweet. Drink your hair.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, what's all this stuff?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, they're brochures from different adoption agencies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, babies! Oh, this one is so cute, get this one!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not really how it works.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, how does it work?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you're having a hard time, you should talk to my friends, Bill and Colleen. They adopted a kid. I'm sure they'd help you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks, that would be great. Hey, honey, wouldn't that be great?
Chandler looks like he did the time he swallowed the toy in 605 TOW Joey's Porsche. It's the hair in his coffee.
Frank Jr. and the triplets enter.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Alright, alright, alright. Remember what we talked about. When we're in a public place, there are certain rules.
The triplets scream and run amok in the coffeehouse.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: That's not what we talked about!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Good to see you, too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Frank.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Hi, how you doin'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, my goodness, they've all gotten so big!
Little Chandler is pulling Chandler's sweater, while Leslie is throwing bagels at him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which one is which again?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, that's Frank Jr. Jr. pulling the tampons out of the lady's purse. And that's Chandler climbing on Chandler, and that's Leslie throwing bagels at him.
Monica (reads a form in her lap): "Willing to adopt triplets?" No!
The hallway in Ross's building. Joey and Rachel are on their way to Ross's dinner.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, can I just say I know we're doing this for Ross, and that's cool, but if it was up to me, this is not what we'd be doing on our first date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what would we be doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'd take you out for a romantic night. Some champagne, fancy dinner, feel you up on the carriage ride home...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Feel me up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In a carriage!
Charlie walks up to them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Charlie!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hi, hi. So.. Dreading this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you bet.
Joey sees that she's carrying a small red bag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, did you bring a little something for Ross?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually.. It's stuff you left at my apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Oh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: And you know, you can just give me my stuff whenever you want.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I didn't throw any of that out..
They enter Ross's apartment. Ross is already quite hyper.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I thought I heard voices! Hi Charlie! (Kisses her.) Hi Joey. (Hugs him.) And.. Oh! You're gonna have to introduce me to your new girlfriend. (Laughs.) I'm just kidding, I know Rachel, I know. (He squeezes her hand.) Come, please come in. Come in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well, we brought you some wine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! That is so thoughtful. (To Joey.) She's a keeper. And what did you bring me? (Grabs the bag that Charlie brought for Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, actually, that's..

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Underwear, a toothbrush, and Van Halen CD. I can use all these things!!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Gosh, Ross, you know, you seem a little...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Fine? Because I am! Aren't you? Aren't you? Aren't you? You see? Who else is fine?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, listen, hey, Ross. Why don't you try to relax, okay? Maybe have a drink.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what? That is a very good idea. I'm gonna go make a pitcher of Margaritas.
Does a Mexican dancing-thing before going to the kitchen.
Central Perk. Frank Jr., Phoebe and the triplets are there. The triplets are now sleeping on top of each other on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, god. So adorable. Look at them sleeping there like angels.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Yeah, I really cherish these moments, 'cause before you know it, they're gonna be awake again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, they may be a handful, but they're so cute.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, god, the last time I babysat them, they did the funniest thing..

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: I haven't slept in four years!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's a, that's a long time.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: You just don't know how hard it is, Phoebe. There's just so many of them. You know, two I can handle. Two's great. You just hold one in each hand, but what do I do when the third one runs at me with his bike helmet on. I've got no more hands to protect my area! There's three of them, Phoebe, three!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, Frank. I counted them when they were coming out of my area.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Sometimes I think that.. Oh, no, no, no, I can't say it, it's too horrible. No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: No, I can't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god, Frank, are you thinking of leaving? Because I didn't have those triplets so you could just run out on them!

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, no! I would never do that. No. I just was thinking that, you know, maybe you could take one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! You can't separate them! That's terrible. Which one?
Bill and Colleen's apartment. Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you so much for seeing us. Phoebe has told us such great things about you guys.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: Oh, please, we're happy to help.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: We went through the same thing when we were adopting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, a lot of malfunctioning wee-wees and hoo-hoos in this room, huh?
Bill and Colleen look shocked.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I mean, you have a lovely home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we appreciate anything you can tell us.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: Well, actually, I think this might help.
She gives Monica a big binder that's perfectly in order.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: It's pretty much all the information you need.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god!

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: Everything is broken down into categories, and then cross-referenced, and then colour-coded to correspond with the forms in the back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. (To Chandler.) I think I just had a tiny orgasm.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: I know the process is frustrating, but it's so worth it. Adopting Owen was the best thing that ever happened to us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great. (To Monica.) Can I see the book?
Monica looks at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You want me to wash my hands first, don't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's.. It's just so pretty and white.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: The bathroom is down the hall, to your left.
Chandler leaves.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: I would have told him to do it too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can I adopt you?
Cut to the hall. Owen is wearing his scout-uniform and is looking through a box when Chandler walks up to him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you must be Owen.

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm Chandler. Hey, I was in the scouts too.

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: You were?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, in fact my father was a den-mother.

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know how to use a compass?

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: I have a badge in it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do? That's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: You wanna see it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd love to, but I gotta get back to talking to your parents. They're telling us all about how they adopted you.

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: What?!?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: I'm adopted?
Chandler tries to come up with a good answer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got nothing.
Ross's apartment. Ross is totally wasted, but he's still drinking all the margaritas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The first batch of margaritas was not so great, but the second batch is gooooood.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe the next batch, we could all get some.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, guys, this is fun, isn't it? You know? Just the four of us. Just hangin'.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, are you okay? And when are the fajitas gonna be ready?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm fine! Hey, I'm great! I'm just.. I'm just proud of us. There's no weirdness, no tension.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No awareness.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We make a great foursome. We should do more stuff together. Ooh! Let's take a trip. Okay, where do you think we - we can go?
The oven timer pings in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My fajitas!!
He runs off to the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, Charlie, I just want you to know. Ross is just having a little trouble adjusting to the thought of Joey and me. You know, he normally doesn't drink like this.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, you know what? This is nothing. My father is a raging alcoholic.
Joey and Rachel don't know how to respond to that.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, have I made this evening uncomfortable?
Ross enters carrying a frying pan with fajitas - without any oven mitts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fajitas! Be careful, very hot plate, very hot plate!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you don't even have oven mitts on!
Ross laughs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That is gonna hurt tomorrow!
Central Perk. Frank Jr., Phoebe and the triplets are there. Continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well, this is crazy. Can't seriously be talking about me taking one of your kids, can we?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: No, of course we're not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Insane.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alice would never go for it, right?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, she's pretty tired, too, I think we've got her onboard.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, just you know, for argument's sake, you know, hypothetically. Which one would you be willing to give up?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Frank Jr.Jr.?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, you'd be getting a really good one. I mean, you know, he's really funny. Like, the other day he made up this joke.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: What's green and says "hey, I'm a frog"? A talking frog! (Laughs.) Oh, no, you can't have him, he's too funny.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, alright, that's fine. What about Leslie?

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, no, no. Not Leslie. No, she's, she's the only one that knows how to burp the alphabet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, so that leaves Chandler.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, no, no, you can't have Chandler, no. No, no. She's my little genius. I got big hopes for her. She's gonna be a doctor or a realtor..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, Frank. I think we just ran out of kids.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: Oh, I think you're right. Oh, wow. Phoebe, I don't think I can give one of them up. I mean, you know, they drive me crazy, but they're my babies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, Frank. I didn't realise things were so bad. You know, I'll help out more. I can - I can babysit any time you want. You name the day, and I'll be there.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: How about tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, that's not good. But you know, I can move some stuff around, and I'll be there. You and Alice just take the whole day together.

lines.speaker: Frank Jr.
lines.text: You'd do that for us?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding? That's what sisters are for.
Frank Jr. (looks at the triplets): Look at them! Aw. I love you so much. (Strokes Leslie's hair, and she moves a little.) Oh crap, don't wake up, don't wake up!
Bill and Colleen's apartment. Chandler comes running into the living room. Monica is the only one there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where are Bill and Colleen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They're in the kitchen getting something to eat. Can you believe how nice they are?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We have to leave!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why? What did you do in the bathroom?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't get to the bathroom. I bumped into Owen on the way, and he didn't know he was adopted. And there's a slight chance I may have told him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, where's my purse? No, you know what? I can replace everything in there. Get that binder, and let's go!
Bill and Colleen enter.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: Some little snacks for everybody. Oh, you don't have to eat the sour worms. Those are for Owen.

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: I'll go get him in a second. By the way, you should know we haven't told him he's adopted yet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But kids are so intuitive. Don't you think on some level he already knows?
Owen comes running in.

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: I'm adopted?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? Intuitive!

lines.speaker: Bill
lines.text: What? Where did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: He told me! And he paid me 50 dollars not to tell.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which technically now you should give back!

lines.speaker: Colleen
lines.text: You told him he's adopted?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm so sorry, but you should have a sign out there or something. Or at least whisper it to people when they come in the door. "Owen doesn't know he's adopted, and he also thinks that Santa is real."

lines.speaker: Owen
lines.text: He isn't?!
Chandler (to Monica): We have to get out of here, baby!
Ross's apartment. Rachel, Joey and Charlie are eating fajitas when Ross enters from the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Everyone? I would like to make a toast to Rachel and Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And to love. Ah, love. L-O-V-E, love. L is for life. And what is life without love?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my god, are we supposed to answer?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: O is for "oh, wow!" The V is for this very surprising turn of events, which I'm still fine with by the way. E is for how extremely normal I find it. That you two are together. And now one day you might get married and have children of your own.
Ross chokes up and pauses. Rachel and Joey look at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Totally.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you don't seem okay.
Ross (on the verge of tears): I'm sorry, it must be the pressure of entertaining. I think everyone would feel better if we had some flan.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Wait, Ross. Ross. I - I have to take off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I'm sorry, I have a really early class in the morning, but this has been lovely.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wasn't it? And you thought it would be awkward with Joey and that you never really liked Rachel.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You're on fire! I'll call you in the morning, okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Alright.
Ross goes to the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: God, Rachel, what Ross just said that is just so..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that's okay, girls tend not to like me.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Bye.
Ross enters from the kitchen with three plates with flan.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I guess it's just flan for three! Hey, hey, that rhymed!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what, Ross? I think we're gonna take off too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, oh. Of course. God, I'm so stupid. You guys are a couple now. I mean, you probably just want to be alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, it's just that it's getting late...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey, it's fine. It's totally fine. We've got plenty of margaritas. It's all good.
The oven timer pings again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't even know what that's for.
He goes back to the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what? I think I'm gonna stay here and make sure he's okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that's probably a good idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I'll see you in the morning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh. Okay. You know what, Joey, I don't think he's ever gonna be okay with this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It doesn't look good, does it?
They kiss each other on the cheek, and Rachel leaves.
Ross's apartment the next morning. Ross is very hung-over on the couch as Joey enters with a cup of coffee for him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Morning. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. Did you stay here all night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you took off my pants and shoes?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no. You actually did that when you were dancing to the Chicago-soundtrack. Look, Ross, about, about Rachel and I. Listen, you don't have to worry about that, okay? Because nothing is gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, she and I said from the beginning that we weren't gonna do anything unless you were okay with it. And clearly..

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what are you talking about? I'm fine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's okay, Ross, alright? I totally understand. Of course you're not fine. You're.. You're Ross and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Except we're not. I mean, we haven't been a couple in like, six years. Oh my god, is that right? Has it been that long?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's what I hear, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is crazy. I mean, six years? And because of me you guys aren't gonna be together? Can I ask you something? Really, what is this thing with you and Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, I mean, you know me, you know...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm crazy about her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And she feels the same way?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, then, maybe it's time we all moved on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but, Ross, I mean, you're not okay with it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but I wanna be. Hey, I will be. Besides, I'm with Charlie, right? Oh my god, I'm still with Charlie, aren't I? I mean, she didn't see the dance, did she?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no, no, that was - that was just for me. Are you sure about this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And we're okay?
Ross smiles and holds up his hand for a high-five, but he has forgotten about his burnt hands. He gasps in pain as Joey grabs his hand.
ENDING CREDITS
Central Perk. Monica and Chandler enter to find Phoebe there with the triplets.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Phoebs

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uhm, we just wanna give you a heads-up. Bill and Colleen hate us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Owen didn't know he was adopted, and Monica told him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Still, he had to find out sometime.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, but how would you like it if someone told the triplets that you gave birth to them?
The triplets stare at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm gonna go tell Emma she was an accident. (Runs off.)
THE END
